candidates.name: openai/codex
candidates.title: OpenAI Codex
candidates.description: Official OpenAI lightweight coding agent that runs in the terminal. Rust-based, Apache-2.0, with 86k+ stars and active daily development.
candidates.signal_summary: Official OpenAI terminal coding agent. 86,110 stars, 12,572 forks, 5,223 open issues. Apache-2.0, created 2025-04, pushed daily. Highest-signal agent framework signal this cycle.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Dominant terminal coding agent reference. Builders comparing or composing agent toolchains need to understand its capabilities, permission model, and limits.

candidates.name: esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
candidates.title: DeepSeek-Reasonix
candidates.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability. TypeScript, MIT license, 10.7k stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 10,703 stars, 593 forks, MIT, pushed 2026-05-27T07:02:11Z. Fast-growing DeepSeek-oriented coding agent with prompt-caching focus.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Strong star velocity and DeepSeek ecosystem relevance. Offers an alternative architecture pattern (prefix-cache stability) worth understanding for agent framework comparisons.
candidates.reason: high star velocity, novel architecture angle

candidates.name: gptme/gptme
candidates.title: gptme
candidates.description: Terminal-based AI agent equipped with local tools: writes code, uses terminal, browses the web. Supports persistent autonomous agents. Python, MIT, 4.3k stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 4,310 stars, 390 forks, MIT, active since 2023. Only 13 open issues, pushed 2026-05-27T07:03:56Z. Mature, actively maintained terminal agent.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Mature terminal-agent pattern with local tool use and persistent agent support. Useful reference for builders designing similar CLI agent experiences.
candidates.reason: mature, low issue count, steady development

candidates.name: rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk
candidates.title: clawd-on-desk
candidates.description: Pixel-art desktop pet that watches Claude Code, Codex, Cursor and other AI coding agents. Electron/JS, AGPL-3.0, 3k stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 3,005 stars, 316 forks, AGPL-3.0, pushed 2026-05-27T07:04:03Z. Novel observability/monitoring concept for coding-agent sessions.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: First-of-kind observability UX for coding-agent sessions. Signals growing demand for agent session visibility and logging tools.

candidates.name: collibra/chip
candidates.title: Collibra MCP Server
candidates.description: Collibra Model Context Protocol server for enterprise data governance. Go, Apache-2.0, 29 stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 29 stars, 5 forks, Apache-2.0, pushed 2026-05-27T06:59:44Z. Enterprise data governance company exposing MCP surface.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Enterprise MCP signal. Collibra is a known data governance brand; builders tracking MCP adoption in regulated domains should note this.
candidates.reason: low-star enterprise MCP; needs license/permissions review

candidates.name: string-os/string
candidates.title: String
candidates.description: Markdown-runs-as-agent-runtime. One file, any agent. TypeScript, MIT, 17 stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 17 stars, 0 forks, MIT, pushed 2026-05-27T07:04:07Z. Experimental markdown-governed agent runtime.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Interesting paradigm: markdown as agent execution substrate. Could influence how agent skills and tool surfaces are defined declaratively.
candidates.reason: novel paradigm but early; sensitive-permissions flag from original scan

candidates.name: Tars-Technologies/cx-agent-evals
candidates.title: CX Agent Evals
candidates.description: Agent evaluation harness focused on customer-experience agents. TypeScript, MIT, 4 stars.
candidates.signal_summary: 4 stars, 0 forks, MIT, pushed 2026-05-27T07:00:10Z. Niche agent evals for CX domain.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Adds to the growing agent-eval ecosystem. Useful reference for builders building eval pipelines.
